sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.54 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: What are the chief factors of a finite group in which every 2-maximal subgroup is not$m$-maximal for any‾$m 3$? A subgroup$H$of a group$G$is said to be$m$-maximal if there is a chain of subgroups$H=H_0<H_1< < H_{m-1}< H_m=G$in which$H_i$is maximal in$H_{i+1}$for every‾$i$. Note that for every$m 3$there is a finite group in which some 2-maximal subgroup is$m$-maximal.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
